data.title: Sweden's Vitrolife Q1 sales fall on currency effects
data.description:
Overview

  • Sweden medical devices and genetic testing firm's Q1 sales rose 5% in local currencies, down 4% in SEK

  • Q1 net income and EPS were flat yr/yr at SEK 101 mln and SEK 0.74

  • Gross margin improved to 59.9% from 57.4% in Q1 last yr

Outlook

  • Vitrolife did not provide specific guidance or outlook for the current or future periods in its press release

Result Drivers

  • REGIONAL PERFORMANCE - Sales growth in local currencies was driven by 11% increase in Americas and 7% in APAC, while EMEA declined 1%

  • PRODUCT GROUPS - Consumables and Technologies sales rose 9% and 11% respectively in local currencies, while Genetics fell 1%

  • GROSS MARGIN IMPROVEMENT - Gross margin increased to 59.9% from 57.4% in Q1 last year

data.title: Finland's Detection Technology Q1 revenue rises 13.5%, but notes uncertainty
data.description:
Overview

  • Finland X-ray detector provider's Q1 2026 revenue grew 13.5% yr/yr

  • Q1 2026 EPS rose to EUR 0.13 from EUR 0.05

  • Company expects net sales to grow yr/yr in Q2 and Q3, but notes uncertainty

Outlook

  • Detection Technology expects net sales to grow year-on-year in Q2 and Q3 2026

  • Company aims for at least 10% annual sales growth and 15% operating margin in medium term

data.title: Spanish stocks - Factors to watch on April 23
data.description:
The following Spanish stocks may be affected by newspaper reports and other factors on Thursday. Reuters has not verified the newspaper reports, and cannot vouch for their accuracy:

BANKINTER

Spain's Bankinter said on Thursday its first-quarter net profit rose 8% from the same period in 2025, helped by a rise in loans and fees.

DIA

DIA said on Thursday its first-quarter total gross sales under banner amounted to 1.76 billion euros.

AIRTIFICIAL

Airtificial said on Thursday it signed a 16-million-euro contract to supply flight control sticks for 30 HURJET aircraft for the Spanish Air Force.

TELEFONICA

Telefonica's CEO, Marc Murtra, has warned nearly 700 senior managers of the need to accelerate execution of the group's Transform & Grow strategy at his first leadership convention since taking office in January 2025, Spanish newspaper Expansion reported on Wednesday.

GRIFOLS

Grifols and U.S. diagnostics firm QuidelOrtho have ended a more than 40-year alliance to produce antigens, with Grifols to receive a $65 million payment while continuing to supply products, Spanish online news website El Economista reported on Thursday.

TUBACEX

Tubacex has warned unions it may seek a temporary layoff scheme due to difficulties stemming from the war in the Middle East, citing oil sector disruptions that could lead to medium-term operational adjustments, Spanish newspaper Expansion reported on Thursday.

data.title: Hold Larsen and Toubro Technology Services; target of Rs 3380: ICICI Securities
data.description:
ICICI Securities research report on Larsen and Toubro Technology Services

LTTS reported a miss on revenue in Q4FY26, given the impact from its divestment of the SWC business. Takeaways: 1) Automotive is stabilising (in line with TELX’s commentary). 2) Continued positive traction in sustainability business (with broad uptick across subsegments). 3) 13–15% revenue CAGR till FY31 for the continuing business under Lakshya 31 program. 4) Completion of restructuring exercise as of Q4FY26. We maintain our neutral stance on LTTS, as execution remains paramount amid a slew of strategy and portfolio changes. While management’s robust 13–15% revenue CAGR outlook gives confidence, we estimate that FY27 revenue growth will likely be in mid-single-digits amid the strategic pivot, leadership changes and macro uncertainty.

Outlook

We maintain our HOLD rating based on an unchanged one-year forward P/E multiple of 22x with a Mar’27E TP of INR 3,380. We cut our FY27–28E EPS by 5-6% to factor in weaker estimated growth in the hi-tech vertical.

Disclaimer

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts/broking houses/rating agencies on moneycontrol.com are their own, and not that of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

LTTS- 2304026 - icici

data.title: Norway's DNB Bank Posts Softer Profit
data.description:
(05:31 GMT) *DnB Bank 1Q Annualized Return on Equity 14.0%
(05:31 GMT) *Analysts Saw DnB Bank 1Q Annualized Return on Equity 13.6%
(05:31 GMT) *DnB Bank 1Q CET1 Ratio 18.1%
(05:32 GMT) *Analysts Saw DnB Bank 1Q CET1 Ratio 18.0%
(05:32 GMT) *DNB Bank 1Q Net Interest Income NOK15.30B
(05:32 GMT) *Analysts Saw DNB Bank 1Q Net Interest Income NOK15.53B
(05:33 GMT) *DnB Bank 1Q Net Commissions, Fees NOK4.13B
(05:33 GMT) *Analysts Saw DnB Bank 1Q Net Commissions, Fees NOK4.27B
(05:33 GMT) *DnB Bank 1Q Rev NOK21.79B
(05:33 GMT) *Analysts Saw DnB Bank 1Q Rev NOK21.78B
(05:33 GMT) *DnB Bank 1Q Net Pft NOK9.86B
(05:33 GMT) *Analysts Saw DnB Bank 1Q Net Pft NOK9.20B
(06:08 GMT) Norway's DNB Bank Posts Softer Profit

By Elena Vardon

Norway's DNB Bank reported a fall in first-quarter profit on higher costs along with a mixed top-line performance.

The lender on Thursday posted 9.86 billion kroner ($1.06 billion) in profit attributable to shareholders for the three-month period, below the 10.85 billion kroner it made in the same period in the prior year.

Total income slipped to 21.79 billion kroner from 21.91 billion kroner, on softer net interest income--the difference between what banks earn on loans and what they pay out on client deposits. The bank noted this was due to repricing, adding that there is also great competition for customers and low activity in housing development.

This was somewhat cushioned by a rise in revenue from fees and commissions for the quarter as DNB benefited from client demand for advice and interest in savings and investment products.

Costs rose nearly 7% for the quarter due to the inclusion of Carnegie, the Swedish investment bank it acquired last year, it added.

"The Norwegian economy has nevertheless proved to be highly resilient during periods of turmoil, and developments in the first quarter confirm this," Chief Executive Kjerstin Braathen said.

DNB's common equity Tier 1 capital ratio--a measure of a bank's financial strength--stood at 18.1%, roughly in line with expectations.

Write to Elena Vardon at elena.vardon@wsj.com

data.title: UK's Oxford Metrics HY revenue rises slightly; keeps FY view
data.description:
Overview

  • UK smart sensing firm estimates half-year revenue rose slightly yr/yr to £20.7 mln

  • Underlying margins remained robust, with improved adjusted LBIT vs prior year period

  • Macroeconomic uncertainty led some Smart Manufacturing customers to defer project starts

Outlook

  • Oxford Metrics maintains FY26 management expectations for the 15-month period ending Dec 2026

  • Company expects efficiency and margin benefits from division integration to build through FY26

  • Oxford Metrics says macroeconomic uncertainty affecting order timing but pipeline supports FY26 delivery
